definition: An Encoder has two main meanings in storage tech: A contraption that translates items into a code, where code can be any sort of signal in any base (bin, hex, other less used ones) or other codes (block event delay), or a contraption that translates a player input (say a selection panel) into code. In addition, in computational redstone, this sometimes refers to decreasing the size of the base used in the code: for example hex to bin. see Transcoder.
